question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFREEZE transaction on-chain targeting their own account-token pair, causing the importer's TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.doUpdateTransaction() to unconditionally set TokenFreezeStatusEnum.FROZEN in the token_account table, and then observe that a subsequent on-chain TOKENUNFREEZE transaction is silently dropped by the importer due to a parsing gap or out-of-order record file, leaving the account permanently frozen and unable to transfer tokens?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.doUpdateTransaction() hardcodes tokenAccount.setAssociated(true) regardless of the actual on-chain association state, so that a crafted TOKENFREEZE transaction against a dissociated account causes the importer to write associated=true with freeze_status=FROZEN, permanently blocking the account from receiving tokens even after a legitimate re-association?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition by submitting a TOKENFREEZE transaction immediately followed by a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction in the same consensus second, such that the importer's SqlEntityListener.mergeTokenAccount() processes the freeze update last due to in-memory merge ordering, resulting in the token_account table permanently reflecting FROZEN status even though the on-chain state is UNFROZEN?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce logic in AbstractTokenAccount's @UpsertColumn for freezeStatus ('case when created_timestamp is not null then {0} else coalesce({0}, e_{0}) end') by submitting a token association transaction that sets created_timestamp, causing a subsequent freeze event's FROZEN status to be overwritten by the coalesce fallback to the old UNFROZEN value, or vice versa, permanently misrepresenting the freeze state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent fund freezing by submitting a TOKENFREEZE transaction that the importer processes successfully (recordItem.isSuccessful() == true), then submitting a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction that fails on-chain (e.g., due to insufficient fee), so the importer's TokenUnfreezeTransactionHandler skips the update because !recordItem.isSuccessful(), leaving the account permanently frozen with no way for the user to unfreeze without the token's freeze key?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SqlEntityListener.mergeTokenAccount() logic where, when lastTokenAccount.hasHistory() is false and newTokenAccount.hasHistory() is true, the freeze status from newTokenAccount is copied to lastTokenAccount without checking whether the new freeze status is FROZEN, causing a stale FROZEN status to persist in the live token_account row even after an unfreeze event was processed in a prior record file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to permanently record FROZEN status for their token account by submitting a TOKENFREEZE transaction during a period when the importer's entityProperties.getPersist().isTokens() flag is toggled off and then back on, causing the freeze event to be skipped but a subsequent balance update to propagate the stale FROZEN status from the existing database row via the coalesce upsert logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenAccountBalanceMigration's UPDATE_TOKEN_ACCOUNT_SQL, which inserts token_account rows with created_timestamp=0 and timestamp_range='[0,)' without setting freeze_status, causing the coalesce(ta.freeze_status, t.freeze_status) in TokenAccountRepository.findById() to fall back to the token-level freeze_status (which may be FROZEN if freezeDefault=true), permanently blocking transfers for accounts that were legitimately unfrozen before the migration ran?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of on-chain transactions (associate, freeze, dissociate, re-associate) such that the importer's SqlEntityListener.mergeTokenAccount() propagates the FROZEN status from the pre-dissociation history row into the new association row, because mergeTokenAccount copies freezeStatus from lastTokenAccount when newTokenAccount.getFreezeStatus() is null, permanently freezing the re-associated account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.doUpdateTransaction() calls entityListener.onTokenAccount() with a TokenAccount that has no createdTimestamp set (null), causing the upsert coalesce logic to treat it as a partial update and merge the FROZEN status into an existing row that was previously UNFROZEN, without creating a proper history entry, making the freeze appear to have always been in effect?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a permanent freeze by submitting a TOKENFREEZE transaction whose consensus timestamp is equal to an existing token_account row's timestampLower, causing the SqlEntityListener.mergeTokenAccount() duplicate-detection branch ('The token accounts are for the same range, accept the previous one') to silently discard a subsequent TOKENUNFREEZE event that shares the same timestamp, leaving the account frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ImporterApplication's @SpringBootApplication component scan to observe that the importer auto-configures all beans under org.hiero.mirror.importer, and by submitting crafted record stream data (if the user controls a node or can inject into the stream), cause the TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to process a synthetic TOKENFREEZE transaction that sets FROZEN on an arbitrary account without a corresponding on-chain freeze key authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent fund freezing by exploiting the fact that the importer's DateRangeCalculator uses STARTUP_TIME as the default start date when no database records exist, causing the importer to skip processing historical TOKENUNFREEZE transactions that occurred before startup, while still processing a TOKENFREEZE transaction that was included in the first downloaded record file, leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FixAirdropTokenAssociationMigration's PATCH_TOKEN_ACCOUNT_SQL, which inserts token_account rows with freeze_status=null and kyc_status=null, causing the TokenAccountRepository.findById() coalesce to return the token-level freeze_status (FROZEN if freezeDefault=true) for accounts that were explicitly unfrozen before the airdrop claim, permanently blocking token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token association transaction that triggers insertAutomaticTokenAssociations() in EntityRecordItemListener, which creates a TokenAccount with no freezeStatus set, and then submit a TOKENFREEZE transaction in the same record file batch, causing the mergeTokenAccount() to propagate FROZEN into the auto-association row without a history entry, making the freeze appear to predate the association?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenFreezeTransactionHandler.getEntity() extracts the account from recordItem.getTransactionBody().getTokenFreeze().getAccount(), and if the protobuf account field is zero/default, EntityId.of() returns EntityId.EMPTY, causing the importer to write a FROZEN status to account ID 0 which may collide with system accounts or cause unexpected behavior in downstream freeze status lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent freezing by exploiting the importer's Flyway migration V1.94.0 which sets freeze_status based on freeze_key presence and freezeDefault, such that a token with freezeDefault=true gets freeze_status=FROZEN (ordinal 1) in the token table, and the coalesce(ta.freeze_status, t.freeze_status) in TokenAccountRepository.findById() returns FROZEN for all token_account rows where freeze_status is null, even for accounts that were explicitly unfrozen on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mergeTokenAccount() logic where, when both lastTokenAccount and newTokenAccount have history, the code copies createdTimestamp and balance from lastTokenAccount to newTokenAccount but only conditionally copies freezeStatus ('if (newTokenAccount.getFreezeStatus() == null)'), so that a TOKENUNFREEZE event with a null freezeStatus (due to a parsing bug) silently inherits the FROZEN status from the previous snapshot, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFREEZE transaction targeting a token with no freeze key (freezeStatus=NOT_APPLICABLE), causing the importer's TokenFreezeTransactionHandler to unconditionally set FROZEN regardless of the token's freeze key presence, creating a discrepancy between the on-chain state (where the freeze would be rejected) and the mirror node's database state, causing downstream applications to incorrectly block transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the importer's batch upsert mechanism (BatchUpserter) where multiple token account updates are batched and the upsert SQL uses 'case when created_timestamp is not null then {0} else coalesce({0}, e_{0}) end' for freeze_status, such that a TOKENFREEZE event processed in the same batch as a re-association event causes the FROZEN status to be written as the initial freeze_status for the new association, permanently freezing the account from creation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause permanent fund freezing by exploiting the ImporterProperties.startDate configuration, where if the importer is restarted with a startDate that skips over a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction but includes a prior TOKENFREEZE transaction, the importer will process the freeze but not the unfreeze, leaving the account permanently frozen in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the importer's CommonConfiguration.dataSource() uses HikariCP connection pooling, and by submitting a high volume of TOKENFREEZE transactions that saturate the connection pool, cause TOKENUNFREEZE transactions to time out during processing, resulting in the freeze being committed but the unfreeze being rolled back, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TokenAccountBalanceMigration's SQL which uses 'on conflict (account_id, token_id) do update set balance = excluded.balance, balance_timestamp = excluded.balance_timestamp' without updating freeze_status, causing a migration run to overwrite a token_account row's timestamp_range to '[0,)' while leaving a stale FROZEN freeze_status from a previous partial migration, permanently blocking transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the importer's @SpringBootApplication enables component scanning of all beans under org.hiero.mirror.importer, including ImporterConfiguration which runs @PostConstruct init(), and by observing that both block source and record downloader cannot be enabled simultaneously, craft a scenario where switching between sources causes the importer to reprocess a TOKENFREEZE transaction without reprocessing the corresponding TOKENUNFREEZE, permanently freezing the account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SqlEntityListener.onTokenAccount() method's claim-handling logic, where if tokenAccount.isClaim() is true and the account is already associated, the update is silently dropped, to cause a TOKENUNFREEZE event that is incorrectly flagged as a claim to be discarded, leaving the account permanently frozen?
